title: Custom web application development | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)

seo.title: Custom web application development | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
seo.description: I design and build custom web applications for companies that need tailored workflows, internal logic, integrations, and room for long-term growth.

page.title: Custom web application development | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: Custom web application development for business-critical workflows
page.description: I design and build custom web applications for companies that need tailored workflows, internal logic, integrations, and room for long-term growth.

hero.title: Custom software when off-the-shelf tools create more friction than value
hero.subtitle: Best fit for portals, internal systems, operational apps, and workflows that depend on company-specific rules, data, and integrations.

intro: Custom development makes sense when a company needs software shaped around its real process instead of shaping the process around a generic tool.

The work is not only about shipping features. The important part is getting the structure right: data model, permissions, integration boundaries, maintainability, and an application that can grow without constant rework.
I can help from scoping and architecture through delivery and ongoing improvement, or join an existing team as a senior contractor on a defined workstream.

sections.title: Where this service is the right fit
body: The strongest fit is a process with multiple roles, approvals, domain-specific rules, internal reporting, or a need to combine data from several systems into one operating view.

sections.title: What I typically handle and deliver
body: This usually includes architecture, full-stack implementation, integration work, release discipline, and the technical choices needed for long-term operation.

sections.title: How the work is run
body: We start by defining the business situation, current constraints, and the first valuable release. Then we deliver in smaller steps so decisions stay tied to real usage and real priorities.

sections.title: What the engagement should achieve
body: The outcome should be a production system that supports the business, stays maintainable, and gives the buyer confidence in future phases.
bullets: less manual work around the product
better long-term ownership than a patchwork stack

faq.question: Do we need a full specification before starting?
faq.answer: No. A clear business problem, the current workflow, and the intended outcome are enough to start discovery and shape a realistic first phase.

faq.question: Can you work inside an existing team?
faq.answer: Yes. I can deliver independently or plug into an existing product or engineering team as a senior contract developer with ownership of a defined area.

faq.question: Can we start with an MVP?
faq.answer: Yes. For larger initiatives, a well-scoped MVP is often the safest way to validate priorities without overbuilding the first release.

faq.question: Do you stay involved after launch?
faq.answer: Yes. Ongoing support can cover improvements, bug fixing, technical debt reduction, performance work, and structured next-phase planning.

for: teams that need tailored software instead of forced SaaS compromises
buyers planning for long-term product ownership
notFor: one-off microsites with no application logic
projects driven only by lowest-cost bidding

cta.label: Discuss your project
cta.note: Share the business context, expected outcome, and current constraints. I will tell you whether the project is a fit.
